name: Gdansk
summary: Gdansk is a colourful old harbour city on Poland's Baltic coast. It is famous for golden amber washed up on its beaches, its tall painted merchant houses and its big medieval wooden crane on the river.
funFacts: Baltic amber is sticky tree sap that hardened into 'gold of the north' over 40 million years, and sometimes hides ancient insects trapped inside.
World War II began in Gdansk on 1 September 1939 when German guns fired on a tiny Polish base called Westerplatte at the harbour mouth.
A peaceful workers' movement called Solidarity started at Gdansk's shipyard in 1980 and grew to over ten million members across Poland.
The bronze sea-god Neptune has stood guarding Long Market since 1617, making him the oldest secular statue in all of Poland.
The wooden Gdansk Crane was the biggest port crane in medieval Europe, lifted by workers walking inside huge wooden treadwheels.
thingsToDo: Wander Long Market and pose with Neptune's Fountain in front of rainbow-coloured merchant houses.
Stand under the giant wooden Gdansk Crane beside the Motlawa River and peek inside its medieval treadwheels.
Hunt for amber treasure on cobbled Mariacka Street, lined with stone porches and quirky dragon gargoyles.
Take a pirate-style river cruise from the Green Gate out to the Westerplatte memorial at the harbour mouth.
Visit the European Solidarity Centre in its rust-red building at the old shipyard gate.
Catch a tram out to Oliwa Cathedral to hear its huge organ playing with moving wooden angels.

wildlife.name: White Stork
wildlife.description: Tall white-and-black bird with a red beak that builds huge stick nests on Polish chimneys.

wildlife.name: European Bison
wildlife.description: Shaggy brown giant of Poland's old forests and Europe's heaviest wild land animal.

wildlife.name: Eurasian Beaver
wildlife.description: Furry river engineer with orange teeth and a flat tail that builds dams from logs.

wildlife.name: Grey Seal
wildlife.description: Whiskery sea mammal with spotty fur that hauls out on Baltic beaches to rest and pup.

localWord.meaning: Hi! A friendly Polish hello, said like 'cheshch'.

images.alt: The colourful row of tall, gabled merchant houses lining Gdansk's Long Market square on a sunny day.
The big wooden medieval Gdansk Crane (Zuraw) standing beside the Motlawa river.
Pretty Mariacka Street in Gdansk's Old Town, lined with stone porches and amber shops.
The rust-coloured steel walls of the European Solidarity Centre museum in Gdansk.
The tall stone Westerplatte Monument that remembers the start of World War II in 1939.
The twin pointed towers of Oliwa Cathedral rising above the trees in Gdansk.
A white stork with a long red beak standing tall in a meadow.
A big shaggy brown European bison, also called a wisent, standing in a Polish reserve.
A Eurasian beaver beside a river dam in northern Poland.
A whiskery grey seal resting on the shore.
